title: statistics: global index has wrong statistics
description: ## Bug Report Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks! ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) `enable-global-index = true; ` in config. ```SQL set @@session.tidb_analyze_version = 1; drop table if exists t; CREATE TABLE t ( a int, b int, c int default 0, key(a) ) PARTITION BY RANGE (a) ( PARTITION p0 VALUES LESS THAN (10), PARTITION p1 VALUES LESS THAN (20), PARTITION p2 VALUES LESS THAN (30), PARTITION p3 VALUES LESS THAN (40)); insert into t(a,b) values (1,1), (2,2), (3,3), (15,15), (25,25), (35,35); alter table t add unique index idx(b); analyze table t; EXPLAIN SELECT b FROM t use index(idx) WHERE b < 16 ORDER BY b; ``` ### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required) ### 3. What did you see instead (Required) ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
poc: CREATE TABLE t ( a int, b int, c int default 0, key(a) )
alter table t add unique index idx(b);
EXPLAIN SELECT b FROM t use index(idx) WHERE b < 16 ORDER BY b;
key_bug_features: enable-global-index in config triggers wrong statistics
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), age INT, salary DECIMAL(10,2) );
INSERT INTO t2 (id, name, age, salary) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30, 50000.00), (2, 'Bob', NULL, 60000.00), (3, 'Charlie', 25, 70000.50);
EXPLAIN SELECT name FROM t2 WHERE age IS NULL OR salary > 55000.00 ORDER BY age DESC;
